item.title: Android Apps Security: Mitigate Hacking Attacks
Git Is Becoming an Execution Surface
item.description: A short note on why branch names, commit metadata, and other Git inputs should be treated as untrusted data once automation, shells, and privileged runtimes start interpreting them.

item.title: Mobuild Envuscator and Digitalis
item.description: Why I built Mobuild Envuscator and Mobuild Digitalis: two tools aimed at the same mobile security problem from different sides.

item.title: Anthesis Update: Memory, Governance, and Beyond the SDLC
item.description: A March 2026 Anthesis update covering the move from expensive one-shot subagent workflows toward memory authority rules, external tool adapters, loop contracts, SSDF integration, Phloem decision records, tracks, gittuf, and session replay.

item.title: Designing Secure Authentication Systems
item.description: Architecture and operational guidance for building authentication systems that limit damage, detect compromise, and recover cleanly.

item.title: Quiet Presence: A Personal Reason for Building a Controlled Booking System
item.description: A personal introduction followed by a technical walkthrough of Quiet Presence: AI-assisted development, static pages, Pages Functions, D1 workflow state, admin-controlled scheduling, and client preference capture.

item.title: Why I Built Entrobert Music
item.description: The reason entrobertmusic.com exists: a practical music side hustle, a way to play with more bands, and a bridge between songwriting, rehearsal work, and audio software.

item.title: Legacy System Insights
item.description: A practical look at legacy systems through the lenses of incentives, trust, incremental change, rewrite risk, and observability.

item.title: Modern Mobile Hardening
item.description: A lifecycle-based model for mobile security that treats the client as hostile and keeps trust anchored in backend policy.

item.title: Threat Modeling AI as an Engineering Coprocessor
item.description: How to treat AI as an untrusted engineering coprocessor and apply supply-chain-grade controls across the SDLC.

item.title: Fatherless: A Book and Movie Trilogy
item.description: The origins, themes, and current state of Fatherless as a combined book and film trilogy project.

item.title: AI SDLC: automating the grind
item.description: Why software delivery feels like a grind loop and how AI can help teams preserve context across the SDLC.

item.title: Software Architecture for Independence
item.description: A short pointer to a longer essay on architectural independence, AI, and security tradeoffs.

item.title: Hockey 2.0
item.description: What I learned returning to hockey as an adult, from skating mechanics to league culture.

item.title: AI and Nano
item.description: How AI could accelerate nanotechnology research, manufacturing, and risk management at the molecular scale.

item.title: Friday Frida Hacking without the Why
item.description: A practical Frida walkthrough showing how rooted Android devices can intercept app behavior at runtime.

item.title: Eyespie (formerly Scouter)
item.description: A prototype image-based social game that evolved through multiple rewrites toward an offline-first, ML-assisted MVP.

item.title: Sandbox Demo
item.description:
A utility from Micrantha for creating reusable isolated development environments.

This is basically an easy to use abstraction around docker with some extra perks.

  • file synchronization that follows symlinks
  • keep package manager and system dependencies isolated from host
  • reusable state that can also be persisted as a ‘sandcastle’ for other sandboxes
  • consistent user file permissions with the host
  • better time tracking with taskwarrior timewarrior scripts
  • write it how I would want it (did not like other tools)

    Demo video currently unavailable.

item.title: Man-in-the-Middle
item.description: A practical overview of MITM attacks, their network-layer mechanics, and the mitigations that actually matter.

item.title: Mind Full, Ness! Don't Care
item.description:
Mindfulness.

When I first heard the word, my mind said “oh, the geniuses that may be have come up with more politically correct word…. Just like breaking down diagnosis’s to something more friendly”.

Did not think much more about than that, same old, same old.

item.title: Hard Done By
item.description:
I am so “hard done by” (adjective; used, cheated, dejected).

It is like a game of attention, who is the most hard done by?

The winner is also the biggest loser, because they are the most hard done by.

Don’t I get it yet? I am not hard done by. Therefore, I am hard done by.

item.title: Do All It Y
item.description:
The dualistic nature of things, that appears so often on earth (look for them you will see)

Could be based on the polarizing energies of male and female that manifest across mammals and other species.

Do you think the universe cares about good and evil for example? A very popular duality.

item.title: Alternate Keyboard
item.description: Why I switched to Colemak, how I trained it, and why a programmable split keyboard made the change easier.

item.title: Guitar Suite Demos
item.description: An old demo reel from a cross-platform music app project built long before the current stack.

item.title: modd: daemon for reloading
item.description:
One of my favourite tools now is modd.

Why is it so great? It does one job, and it does it well, in proper unix fashion.

Monitoring a file-system and running a daemon that reloads if anything changes.

Also in proper Unix fashion, the daemon is sent a configurable SIGHUP when a file changes.

item.title: Global World Order
item.description:
After some discussions earlier today regarding legality, countries and the internet, had to jot down some thoughts.

The rule of law for governing works at different levels.

Am sure you are aware how globalization and the internet have exposed how difficult it is for countries to integrate with each other without some sort of super world organization governing that.

item.title: Covuck. Pleasantly.
item.description:
Aside from being allowed to have no life or interact with people (which is great btw).

Something really positive about covid-19…

Is something that drives me wild with love 😍 and passion 😘

I am talking about the little extra plumpness to Canadians.

item.title: Fire with Fire
item.description:
Context: American White House Takeover by Protesters (aka racist terrorists)

Do you ever notice that whenever humanity gets squeezed, our worst selves make the most noise?

The mental-emotional state of the human condition, acting like a virus. And we indirectly like to think we are a part of something.

item.title: Who are you?
item.description:
Who are you? Who am I?

Are you a body?

Are you a thought?

Are you more than a pin point in time?

If your body degenerates, are you still your body?

If your thought changes, are you still your thought?

item.title: Opinion Wall
item.description:
1. Opinions are also like onions. They can make others cry, or your “self” when it gets all wrapped in as who you are.

  2. Given the atomic bomb of the “pill”, I think professional sex workers deserve some rights. Its not that I want to use their service, but it just does not make sense to control peoples bodies when we send people to war. What we do need to control is health. I do not trust, Dating Apps, Pimps or the Porn industry to regulate health. I would rather Health Canada to step in for safety and cleanliness.

item.title: Questioning the Queue
item.description:
Do you feel entitled to be a victim of someone or something else?

Do you think your mind or the situation generates more suffering?

Do you think that you deserve better? That you are overlooked?

That your perception of reality is absolute?

item.title: Mornin 'Mericas.
item.description:
Winners and Losers. All of us have probably felt this is true in some level.

Its true with men, and the various games in the lives of the different hues of man.

Have not read Trumpian literature on the subject but do have my own opinion that is somewhat biased to the loser side.

item.title: Covac
item.description:
Am very glad a covid vaccine has a real chance.

“Little bottle of hope” for saving humans and creating prosperity for more (groan, yes, more humans are needed now eh).

That being said, I am actually very bitter going back to the new-normal, with a lot of skepticism.

item.title: The Dreams
item.description: Life is a like a dream, a background awareness of reality.…. as a nightmare at worst and a delusion at best. You can guide your self down the stream, but you do not know where you are going and you cannot go back.

item.title: Poly Techs
item.description:
Politics. Does it matter? Why?

It is great that the human condition is suffering, as there is a plethora of issues to take unconscious action on.

When it comes down to actionable issues that are most common, it is always children.

item.title: Indulgence into the past
item.description:
Hello, World

I am a late bloomer, not finding post-secondary until ~28 years of age.

I have survived in catch-up mode for the last ~12 years, but ultimately has left me with many short straws, and no long ones. In my career, education and personal life.

item.title: Blockchain Mesh Networks
item.description: A look at community mesh networking combined with blockchain incentives for decentralized internet access.

item.title: The Dead Triplets
item.description:
This post could about music theory and rhythm, such as the triplet guitar riffs in invincible…

But this about a single chapter, in a single book from the swamp. It is probably already a more influential book than I realize, from which I quote liberally and implicitly.

item.title: Myths Fading, Direction of Evolution
item.description:
Remembering a concept from the book Sapiens (paraphrasing):

Ape hierarchy only existed in small groups with a limit (150 or so) with a dominant leader (alpha). This applies to humans as well, but we were able so surpass the threshold with myths and better communication.

item.title: Git Notes Storage
item.description: Using git notes as a lightweight place to store build metadata without polluting the main repository history.

item.title: Audit
item.description: A redirect and cleanup stub for older blog URLs that no longer map to standalone posts.

item.title: 12 Bar Blues
item.description:
You might have heard of the blues, made famous as a way to make a living in the US for marginalized citizens.

The story goes that the French taught the Black community music theory and unlocked a creative juice thirsting for expression.

item.title: Mirroring git with jenkins
item.description: Using Jenkins pipeline jobs to mirror git repositories across multiple remotes on a schedule.

item.title: What I've Learned from 4 Years of Eckhart Tolle
item.description:
Major Lessons

Eckhart Tolle or ET is a unique individual, author and orator. After a suicidal night where he thought “I cannot live with myself any longer” and recognized the oddity of thought, as if there were two people “I” and “myself”. That recognition brought a radical inner transformation and years of intense bliss. He later learned more about what happened to him and became a “spiritual teacher”.

item.title: Favourite 'nixisms
item.description: A running list of Unix habits, tools, and conventions that make Unix-like systems feel durable and elegant.

item.title: Deploy Docker Registry with Dokku
item.description:
Prerequisites

You’ll need to prepare a bit.

  1. A local path on the server to store registry data. For example: /mydir/registry
  2. Docker installed on the client and server machine
  3. Dokku installed on the server

For Authentication

  1. httpasswd command is installed

For an External Registry

  1. Dokku letsencrypt plugin installed
  2. A working domain name pointing to the dokku app. For example, https://registry.mydomain.com

Create the dokku app

Create the dokku app and retag a pre-existing repository image to it. To persist data, a volume is used.

item.title: Fortunes on Slack
item.description: A small Go microservice that serves classic Unix fortunes through an HTTP API and a Slack slash command.

item.title: Microsoft Dev Stack on macOS
item.description:
Gonna evangelize a little bit.

When Microsoft bought Xamarin, they did not underestimate the power of Xamarin.Forms for building cross platform UIs. Now with the release of Visual Studio for macOS and the Dotnet Core, its looks like its going to stay alive.

item.title: Hugo + Dokku + Wercker = Goodness
item.description:
Hugo

Hugo is a static site generator with many powerful features like archtypes, themes, customization, etc.

Combined with tools like bower and brunch it makes for a pretty powerful app that could be more than a blog.

The workflow is simple - build your assets with brunch, run the hugo generator. The end result is a complete static html site you can deploy.

item.title: c hacking: polymorphing 2
item.description:
While working on cparse I found another nifty way to sort of simulate polymorphism.

cparse

Another exercise in futility, a C implementation for the REST api at parse.com. Really the core of it is a web client and an object structure using json.

item.title: Elixir and Phoenix
item.description: An experiment building a lightweight chat app in Elixir and Phoenix to explore the Erlang VM.

item.title: Poor Man's Polymorphism
item.description: Found out that C structs can be casted between each other if the field types and ordering are the same. It is not portable code, but you can start too see how and why the virtual table and object oriented programming evolved in c++.

item.title: Crafting a Game With CraftyJS
item.description:
Introduction

Briefly going to mention a javascript game engine called CraftyJS that I’ve been playing with the past couple of days.

Has the usual suspects like Entity Component System, Collision, Sprite Maps, etc.

The Project

item.title: C Sqlite Tricks
item.description:
Introduction

Thought I would share some tricks I use for easy data CRUD’s in C. I’m using Sqlite here, but could apply to any database.

Not saying its the best approach at all (nor ryjen/db), this is just how it evolved for me.

item.title: Best Programming Quotes
item.description: A personal collection of programming quotes about craft, simplicity, bugs, and engineering reality.

Scouter

Alternatively Eye Spy, iSpy, PicPatrol, CamAgent.

Motivations

Mostly to use machine learning and potentially build or strengthen an image classification model.

It is based on the classic game of “I Spy” and a good way to discover new things about your environment.

item.title: About
item.description: Background on Ryan Jennings, his work, interests, and the personal context behind the site.

item.title: Drawings
item.description:
Lake Scene
Gerty: A Portrait
Flow Woman
Rose Blades
Heart Spirals
Amphibian Hand Hair
Osterich Tribe
Owl of Me (Me-Owl)
Osterich Portrait
Crumby Quacker
Gertrude: Don't be Rude
Birch Bark
Worm Cells
Stuntman Missing Noah's Ark
Moth of Her (mother)
Stardum: Moon Eating
Black Hole Brain Flower
Dinosaurus, Rock, Sex Transfusion
Cube Glaze
Droid, Card, Watercolour
Greek Upside Down Smile Pi
Logs
Penelopy: A Rough Trade
'arnie from the (Tan) Club
Clouds, Card, Watercolour
Brain Casing, Cracked
Joker and the Thief
Can, but Nada
Life for Breakfast
Treble Clef: Art in itself
Happiness in Apocolypse
The Other Side
Imp Ear, Real
Portrait (Ana Kao version)

item.title: Illusions: Adventures of a Reluctant Messiah
Music
item.description:
This page is a loose home for recordings, sketches, and a few arrangements that have survived long enough to stay in rotation.

SoundCloud

Sheet Music

MuseScore has a small set of nursery rhymes and Christmas carols that I rearranged into minor keys.

item.title: Summary
item.description:
Summary of Ryan Jennings

Career History, Expertise and Interests

Interests

Ryan has a strong interest in cross-platform mobile development from his experience in PALM, Windows Phone, Blackberry, iOS, Android, Xamarin and React-Native. He has good knowledge of UX principles and is particularity strong at bridging native platform solutions to cross-platform user interfaces.
